Her play would be retatrutide for eight to 16 weeks to get the result, then rotate. Mazdutide gives you glucagon without GIP. Tirzepatide gives you GIP without glucagon. You're varying the inputs. The Ten Commandments Decide what your goal is. Weight loss or longevity. Different plans. Sort out hormones first. TRT, thyroid, sleep, training, protein. Start low. 1 to 2 mg weekly. chronic_signals.snippet: he literature looks like roughly half GLP-1 and half glucagon. Reta is about 20 percent GLP, 20 percent glucagon, 60 percent GIP. That means milligram for milligram you get more GLP-1 from mazdutide,
than to tirzepatide or reta. Mazdutide will be good for fat loss, especially liver fat, because of the glucagon component. But reta still wins on percentage of liver fat removed in the clinical data.
